name: Bread Machine Onion Bagels
recipeInstructions:
1. Presoak 1/4 cup dried onions in 1/3 cup warm water for 10 minutes. Drain and press out excess water. Place the pre-soaked onions in the bread machine with the remaining ingredients. Save some of the onions to sprinkle on top of the bagels before baking.
2. Insert ingredients into the bread machine according to the manufacturer's instructions. Remove the dough from the machine after the first knead, approximately 20 to 30 minutes.
3. Place dough on a floured surface. Divide into 8 pieces. Form balls, gently press thumb through the center of each ball, and slowly stretch into bagel shape.
4. Boil rapidly boiling water (3 quarts) in a large saucepan with 1 tablespoon sugar until it reaches a rapid boil. Drop a test piece of dough into the water.
5. Using a slotted spoon, drop 2-3 bagels into the rapidly boiling water. Boil each side for 30 seconds. Remove and cool on a rack for 1 minute, brush with egg wash (or substitute with an egg wash alternative), and sprinkle with sesame or poppy seeds if desired.
6. Bake at 400°F on a baking sheet sprinkled with cornmeal until golden brown, approximately 15 minutes.
Bagel Success Hints
- When forming the bagels, set aside two small balls of dough. After the bagels have doubled in size, drop them into boiling water. The dough should immediately pop to the top when dropped.
- A quick spray of non-stick vegetable cooking spray on the top of the bagel may be substituted for the egg wash.
- To make bagel sticks, cut the bagels before they rise and lay them out in a straight line. Roll the sticks in a combination of sesame and poppy seeds with a pinch of garlic powder. Let the sticks rise, boil, and bake as described above.
- To make bagel chips, slice leftover bagels horizontally into thin slices. Brush one side with butter or margarine and place (butter side up) on an ungreased cookie sheet. Bake at 325°F for 12-15 minutes until golden brown and crisp.
